pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 37: input post includes main_power; verified through setup_task.sh lines 41 and 49 where input processing is configured for main_power input
setup_task.sh line 37: input post includes ev_charger; verified through setup_task.sh line 42 where EV_INPUT_ID is retrieved for ev_charger input
setup_task.sh line 37: input post includes heat_pump; verified through setup_task.sh line 43 where HP_INPUT_ID is retrieved for heat_pump input
Task description requires creating a feed named 'residual_power'; verifier.py line 56 uses this metadata value; setup scripts reference this feed name
setup_task.sh line 22: feed created with name=main_power; verified through setup_task.sh line 49 where main_power input is configured to log to this feed

Task.json claims tolerance_percent=0.05 (5%), but verifier.py line 77 hardcodes tolerance = abs(main_in * 0.1), implementing 10% tolerance. The tolerance_percent field is defined in metadata but never read or used in verifier.py—it is completely ignored. The actual verification uses 0.1 (10%) not 0.05 (5%).

privileged_info_summary: The task derives residual power (main - EV - heatpump) using Emoncms energy monitoring system. Input names are verified as main_power, ev_charger, and heat_pump, logging to target feed residual_power. Data is synthetic, generated by a Python traffic generator simulating realistic household power consumption (main: 2000-2400W, EV: ~1200W, heat pump: ~800W). Critical discrepancy found: metadata claims tolerance_percent=0.05 (5%), but the actual verifier uses 10% tolerance (hardcoded as main_in * 0.1 on verifier.py line 77). The tolerance_percent metadata field is unused in verification logic.
